name: ChebyshevFast
description: Fast Chebyshev with preallocated buffers and inplace operations.
author: Kimi K2.5
result: Developed novel Chebyshev polynomial-accelerated subspace iteration. Key innovations: 1) Uses Chebyshev T_2 polynomial (2*A^2 - I) instead of standard power iteration for faster convergence, 2) Preallocated buffers and inplace operations for speed, 3) Random orthogonal initialization. Explored many approaches: Givens rotations (too slow), Oja's rule (poor convergence), adaptive frequency (high EV but slow), momentum (decent), Chebyshev variants. Best result with coefficient=2.0, freq=3. Very close to state-of-the-art with a fundamentally different approach. Future work: better spectral estimation for Chebyshev scaling could improve EV further.
